name: testing-patterns
description: Cross-language testing strategies and patterns. Triggers on: test pyramid, unit test, integration test, e2e test, TDD, BDD, test coverage, mocking strategy, test doubles, test isolation.
capabilities: Implement test pyramid with correct unit, integration, and E2E balance
Apply TDD red-green-refactor cycle for new features
Choose appropriate test doubles (stubs, mocks, spies, fakes)
Create test data using fixtures, factories, and builders
Configure CI testing pipelines with parallelization and caching
Analyze test quality and identify anti-patterns
author: 0xDarkMatter
securitySummary: Documentation-focused skill providing testing pattern guidance. Static findings are false positives from markdown code examples. The scanner flagged backtick-delimited code blocks as shell execution, example DATABASE_URLs as credential access, and testing tool names like pytest/docker as reconnaissance. The single utility script (coverage-check.sh) is a benign pytest wrapper with no network access or credential handling.
valueStatement: Writing effective tests requires understanding the right patterns for each scenario. This skill provides universal testing strategies, from the test pyramid to TDD workflows, so you can build reliable test suites.
limitations: Does not execute tests directly in the workspace
Framework-specific implementations are referenced, not provided
Does not generate complete test suites automatically
Coverage script requires pytest and coverage plugins

useCases.title: Learn testing fundamentals
useCases.description: Understand the test pyramid and how to structure tests for different scenarios

useCases.title: Design test strategies
useCases.description: Create comprehensive test plans with proper isolation and mocking approaches

useCases.title: Build CI testing pipelines
useCases.description: Configure efficient testing pipelines with parallelization and coverage gates

promptTemplates.title: Basic unit test
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me write a unit test for [function name] including arrange-act-assert structure and proper mocking of dependencies.

promptTemplates.title: Test pyramid
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a test pyramid for my [feature name]. How many unit, integration, and E2E tests should I have?

promptTemplates.title: TDD workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Guide me through TDD for [feature]. Start with a failing test, then minimal code, then refactor.

promptTemplates.title: Advanced mocking
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up mocking for [external API/database] in my tests. Include stubs for responses and mocks for verifying interactions.

bestPractices: Write tests before production code using TDD for new features
Mock only at external boundaries (databases, APIs, file system)
Keep tests independent with no order dependencies
antiPatterns: Testing implementation details instead of public behavior
Mocking the code under test itself
Writing tests that mirror production code logic

faq.question: Which testing frameworks work with these patterns?
faq.answer: Patterns are language-agnostic. Python (pytest), JavaScript (Jest), Java (JUnit), Go (testing) all apply.

faq.question: What is the recommended coverage threshold?
faq.answer: Aim for 80% unit test coverage. Integration and E2E tests cover critical paths only.

faq.question: How do I integrate with CI pipelines?
faq.answer: Run fast tests (lint, unit) first. Parallelize with matrix builds. Cache dependencies between runs.

faq.question: Is my test data safe?
faq.answer: This skill only provides patterns. Test data handling depends on your test configuration and CI secrets management.

faq.question: Why are my tests flaky?
faq.answer: Flaky tests often have external dependencies, timing issues, or shared state. Isolate and use deterministic data.

faq.question: How is this different from test documentation?
faq.answer: These are actionable patterns you apply directly. The skill helps you choose and implement the right approach.
